title: HR Software Buyer's Guide: How to Choose the Right People Platform
description: A structured framework for evaluating HR platforms across payroll, benefits, time tracking, performance management, and compliance — with weighted criteria and implementation guidance for small to mid-sized businesses.

sections.title: Assess Your HR Workload
sections.body: Start by auditing the time your team spends on HR tasks today. Track hours per month across these categories: payroll processing and tax filing, benefits administration and open enrollment, time-off request management, employee record maintenance, compliance reporting, recruiting and onboarding, and performance review administration. Most growing businesses find that payroll and compliance tasks consume 60-70% of HR hours. This audit reveals which HR software features will deliver the fastest ROI: teams spending 20+ hours per month on payroll should prioritize payroll automation, while teams spending significant time on manual onboarding workflows should evaluate applicant tracking and onboarding features first. Document your pain points before evaluating any vendor so you can score each platform against your actual bottlenecks rather than generic feature checklists.

sections.title: Core HR Features Evaluation
sections.body: HR platforms offer varying depth across core capabilities. Use this framework to evaluate each candidate, adjusting weights based on your workload audit results.
rows: Auto tax filing, multi-state support, W-2/1099 generation, garnishment handling, payroll calendar flexibility
Health insurance integration, 401k deduction sync, HSA/FSA management, self-service enrollment portal
Paid time off tracking, sick leave policies, time clock integration, overtime calculation, vacation accrual rules
EEO-1 reporting, ACA tracking, state-specific labor law updates, audit trail, document retention policies
Performance review templates, goal tracking, 360 feedback, succession planning, skills inventory
Self-service portal, mobile app quality, org chart visualization, company directory, announcement tools

sections.title: Payroll Depth Matters Most
sections.body: Payroll is the highest-risk HR function because errors trigger tax penalties and employee dissatisfaction. Test each platform's payroll engine with your specific scenarios: multi-state payroll for remote teams across different tax jurisdictions, wage garnishment processing (child support, tax levies, creditor garnishments), contractor payments with 1099-NEC filing, commission and bonus calculations that vary by role, and retroactive pay adjustments for timesheet corrections. Gusto handles these well for teams under 50 employees with transparent pricing and auto tax filing that includes a tax penalty guarantee. BambooHR integrates with third-party payroll providers like Gusto and ADP rather than offering native payroll, which adds integration complexity but lets you choose best-in-class payroll separately. For this reason, BambooHR often suits organizations that already have a payroll provider and need better HR tracking, while Gusto works best as an all-in-one for companies that want payroll and HR unified.

sections.title: Compliance Requirements
sections.body: HR compliance requirements vary significantly by company size, industry, and geographic footprint. Verify that any platform you evaluate supports your specific compliance obligations: federal reporting (EEO-1 for companies with 100+ employees, ACA reporting for applicable large employers, OSHA recordkeeping if required), state-specific requirements (California's paid sick leave and CFRA, New York's paid family leave, Colorado's overtime and meal break rules among the most complex), multi-state tax registration and withholding for remote teams, and industry-specific regulations (HIPAA for healthcare employers, Davis-Bacon for government contractors). Ask each vendor how they stay current with legislation changes — most reputable HR platforms include automatic compliance updates in their subscription, but some charge extra for compliance modules or delay updates by weeks.

sections.title: Integration Requirements
sections.body: An HR platform is only as useful as its connections to your existing tools. The most critical integrations to verify are: accounting software (QuickBooks, Xero for GL coding of payroll expenses and benefits deductions), benefits providers (health insurance carriers, 401k administrators, commuter benefit vendors), recruiting tools and job boards (LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor for automated posting and candidate flow), time tracking systems (if using a third-party time clock instead of the HR platform's native option), and identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace for automated user provisioning through SCIM). Test integration data flow during your trial — some HR platform integrations only sync employee records and not pay data, which means you still need manual journal entries in your accounting system. For companies with complex tech stacks, BambooHR's open API and marketplace of 100+ pre-built integrations often provides more flexibility than Gusto's more curated integration set.

sections.title: Common HR Software Selection Mistakes
sections.body: Avoid these pitfalls that frequently derail HR platform implementations and waste months of team effort.
items: Choosing based on payroll price per employee without factoring in implementation costs, data migration effort, and ongoing admin time for tasks that remain manual
Overlooking multi-state tax compliance for remote teams — many platforms charge extra per state or limit the number of state tax profiles on lower-tier plans
Assuming benefits administration is included — many HR platforms handle employee records but require separate benefits broker software for carrier connections and enrollment
Not testing the mobile experience for employee self-service — if your workforce is deskless or field-based, a weak mobile app creates constant support tickets for HR
Signing a multi-year contract before running a full payroll cycle with real employee data — payroll accuracy can only be verified with actual calculations, not demos
Failing to involve the finance team in evaluation — payroll integrations with accounting systems affect month-end close timelines and reconciliation processes

sections.title: Budget Framework
sections.body: HR platform costs include more than the per-employee subscription fee. Calculate total annual cost using this framework: base subscription (monthly fee × number of employees × 12), payroll processing fees (per-payrun or per-employee-per-pay period, varies by frequency), benefits administration add-ons (per employee per month if not included in base), integration costs (Zapier subscriptions, API credits, or middleware fees), implementation and data migration (vendor onboarding fees range from $500-5,000 for small businesses), and ongoing administration (HRIS admin time for user changes, compliance updates, and reporting). Typical ranges: all-in-one HR platforms like Gusto run $40-80 per employee per month for full payroll, benefits, and HR features. Core HRIS platforms like BambooHR run $6-12 per employee per month plus separate payroll costs. Enterprise HR suites like Rippling or Workday scale from $15-50 per employee per month depending on module count.

sections.title: Decision Checklist
sections.body: Before signing an HR platform contract, work through this checklist with your HR lead and finance team.
items: Have you run at least one full payroll cycle with real employee data in the platform?
Does the platform support all states where your employees work, including local tax jurisdictions?
Can your accounting software receive payroll journal entries automatically without manual data entry?
Does the mobile app allow employees to request time off, view pay stubs, and update personal information?
Have you verified that the platform handles your specific benefit deduction types (health, 401k, HSA, FSA)?
What is the cost and process for adding new states as your team grows geographically?
Does the vendor provide a tax penalty guarantee that covers IRS fines from software filing errors?
How long does support take to respond to time-sensitive issues during payroll processing?
Is employee data export available in a standard format if you decide to switch platforms later?

sections.title: Implementation Timeline
sections.body: Plan for a 6-8 week implementation timeline from contract signing to first live payroll run. Week 1-2: Set up company policies, pay schedules, benefits plans, and time-off policies with the HR admin. Load employee data including salary and wage information, tax withholdings, and direct deposit details. Week 3-4: Connect integrations with accounting software, benefits carriers, and time tracking systems. Run a parallel payroll test comparing platform output against your current payroll calculations. Week 5-6: Migrate historical employee records, completed onboarding for all employees, and conduct admin training. Run a second parallel payroll cycle to verify accuracy. Week 7-8: Launch the first live payroll run with a rollback plan that keeps your previous system accessible. Schedule a 30-day and 60-day checkpoint to address any workflow gaps or training needs that emerged after the initial launch.

author: StackPilot Team
